metadata.title: Below-threshold & out-of-scope risk pools — credit unions, private credit/NDFIs, ILCs, the Farm Credit System, foreign-bank US branches, and the suppressed FDIC problem-bank total
metadata.frame: The FDIC bank aggregate is one slice of US credit. This block maps the comparable HTM/CRE/duration/credit risk that sits OUTSIDE or BELOW that lens - a different regulator, a smaller size cut, or no public disclosure at all - so the 'banks look clean' read isn't mistaken for 'the system is clean.' Real figures where public; flagged where opaque. Companion to [[macro-fdic]] (the coverage-gaps section) and [[macro-bank-htm-marks]] (the per-bank tail) and [[macro-cre-privatecredit]] (the NDFI chain).
metadata.relevance: Closes the coverage-gap dig from [[macro-fdic]] section 5; stacks the parallel pools against the FDIC bank number; feeds the self-marked-value and CRE/duration threads with the uncovered reservoirs.
metadata.sources: NCUA Quarterly Data Summaries / press (2025); Ceto (CU failures); Fed FEDS note + NY Fed Teller Window + Morgan Stanley/With Intelligence (private credit/BDC); CRS R46489 + Wikipedia + ICBA (ILCs); Farm Credit Funding / FCS combined results (FCS); Federal Reserve IBA release (foreign-bank branches); Wolf Street (FDIC problem-bank-total suppression). Full URLs in the rendered .md.

credit_unions.summary: A ~$2.43T parallel banking system under a DIFFERENT regulator (NCUA), entirely absent from FDIC data - and now in its worst failure cluster in years.

points.detail: Federally-insured credit unions held ~$2.43T in assets at Q4 2025 (+$126B / +5.4% YoY); aggregate net-worth ratio ~11.26% (up YoY) - well-capitalized in aggregate.
Delinquency climbed through 2025: 91bp (Q2) -> 95bp (Q3) -> 103bp (Q4), up ~5bp YoY; CRE/non-commercial-RE delinquency ~88bp (Q4, +10bp YoY).

6 credit-union failures Apr-Aug 2025 - the largest cluster in ~7 years, ending a near-two-year stretch with no liquidations; all small (largest ~$58.5M) with weak profitability + deteriorating credit + governance gaps.

CUs file NCUA Call Report Form 5300 (not FDIC), carry their own HTM/AFS unrealized securities losses and CRE/member-business-lending concentration, and are CECL-covered - none of it in the FDIC aggregate the bubble thesis usually cites.

private_credit_ndfi.summary: The largest uncovered reservoir - the risk that MIGRATED off bank balance sheets, now ~$1.7-3T and the home of AI-datacenter financing.

points.detail: US private credit ~$1.7T (some scopes $2.3-3T at start-2025), projected ~$5T by 2029 (Morgan Stanley) - up ~5x since 2009.

BDCs (listed + non-traded) ~$500B; private-wealth vehicles (BDCs/interval/tender funds) >$400B; evergreen private-credit funds ~$644B (Jun 2025, +28% H1). Manager-set NAVs - the third self-marked number ([[macro-private-credit-marks]]).
Banks lend to NDFIs ~$1.97T (Fed) - so bank risk didn't vanish, it moved one step out and became opaque; this is exactly where AI-datacenter SPV financing lives ([[macro-cre-privatecredit]]).

industrial_loan_companies.summary: FDIC-INSURED but Fed-UNSUPERVISED - the charter loophole that lets commercial/fintech firms own a bank without consolidated supervision.

points.detail: Total ILC assets ~$247.7B (2025), up from ~$25.1B (1997); ~23 active charters; 6 ILCs over $10B; the largest ~$116.3B.

ILCs are FDIC-insured yet their commercial parents escape Federal Reserve consolidated (bank-holding-company) supervision - the basis of the ICBA's 'close the loophole' campaign and the fintech (Square/etc.) and retail/auto interest in the charter.

farm_credit_system.summary: A federal GSE lending complex for agriculture - off the FDIC map, exposed to farmland values and ag commodity cycles.

points.detail: The Farm Credit System (a government-sponsored enterprise, funded via the Federal Farm Credit Banks Funding Corp) held roughly ~$188B farm real-estate loans + ~$81B non-real-estate farm loans at year-end 2024 (a ~$400B+ asset GSE).
Concentrated in farmland values and ag commodity cycles; a farmland-price correction or a trade-war ag shock is its tail - distinct from the bank/CRE map.

foreign_bank_us_branches.summary: US branches/agencies of foreign banks - a large dollar-funding/repo node tracked by the Fed, separate from the FDIC bank set.

points.detail: US branches and agencies of foreign banking organizations hold ~$2T+ in US assets (Fed IBA release), heavily involved in wholesale dollar funding and repo - a transmission node for offshore-dollar stress, not in the FDIC domestic-bank aggregate.
points.grade: fact (Fed IBA); exact total in the release

suppressed_problem_bank_total.summary: Even within FDIC scope, the size of the troubled set is now hidden.

points.detail: Since Q4 2024 - the first time since 1990 - the FDIC publishes only the problem-bank COUNT (~54), not the ASSET total, citing the risk that disclosure could trigger a 'disorderly run.' The distribution is deliberately below disclosure; the per-bank reconstruction in [[macro-bank-htm-marks]] partly recovers it.

total_stack_tally.summary: Ten-plus multi-trillion pools sit outside or below the FDIC bank lens - and many share the SAME underlying collateral (Treasuries, agency MBS, private-credit NAVs), so the danger is correlation, not a clean sum.
total_stack_tally.deep_dig_blocks: Each pool below is now its own block: [[macro-money-market-funds]], [[macro-treasury-basis-trade]], [[macro-pensions-ldi]], [[macro-gses-fhlb]], [[macro-family-offices]], [[macro-fintech-baas]], [[macro-mortgage-reits]], [[macro-consumer-abs-subprime]], [[macro-municipal-public-finance]] - plus credit unions, private credit/NDFIs, ILCs, the Farm Credit System and foreign-bank US branches above.

pools.lens_gap: non-bank cash pool; constant-NAV, run-prone, no FDIC; dominates repo + ~40% of T-bills
quasi-sovereign housing finance on thin capital (~43x); FHLB super-lien ahead of FDIC
different regulator (NCUA); own HTM/CRE; 7-yr-high failure cluster
near-zero-haircut repo leverage in the $30T Treasury market; top-10 ~18:1
adviser-registration exemption; swap-hidden leverage (Archegos); 10B-1 fix withdrawn
leveraged LDI (UK 2022 doom loop); self-marked alts; net outflow
repo-funded ~6-9x on the same MBS banks/Fed hold; 2020 fire-sale precedent

pools.size: slice of ~$18.8T household debt; ~$358B 2025 ABS; ~$400B invisible BNPL
pools.lens_gap: off-balance-sheet securitization; subprime tail at 32-yr highs; collateral fraud (Tricolor/First Brands)
GSE off the FDIC map; farmland/ag-commodity exposure

pools.size: tech-scale deposits on small sponsor banks
pools.lens_gap: 'FDIC-insured' conditional on a private ledger (Synapse: ~$85-96M lost)

total_stack_tally.honest_tally: Adding only the cleanly-sized, low-overlap pools (MMFs ~$7.9T, agency/GSE complex ~$11T, munis ~$4.2T, credit unions ~$2.43T, private credit ~$1.7-3T, foreign-bank branches ~$2T+, family offices ~$3.1T, ILCs ~$248B, Farm Credit ~$400B+) gives an order of magnitude of ~$33-37T of credit/duration risk sitting OUTSIDE the FDIC domestic-bank deposit lens - itself comparable to the ~$24T FDIC-insured bank asset base. This is an order-of-magnitude floor, NOT a precise sum: the pools overlap heavily (MMFs/hedge funds/pensions all hold the same Treasuries; mREITs/banks/Fed hold the same agency MBS; pensions hold the private credit), so the true systemic point is the SHARED collateral, not the dollar total.
total_stack_tally.grade: labeled interpretation (order of magnitude; explicit double-counting caveat)

synthesis.summary: Stack the pools: the FDIC bank number is a floor, not the system.

points.detail: Beyond the FDIC bank aggregate sit a dozen-plus multi-trillion pools (see total_stack_tally): ~$7.9T money market funds, an ~$11T GSE/FHLB/agency complex, ~$4.2T munis, ~$2.43T credit unions, ~$1.7-3T private credit/NDFIs, ~$2T+ foreign-bank branches, ~$3.1T family offices, leveraged pensions/LDI, repo-funded mortgage REITs, the consumer-ABS/subprime tail, ~$248B ILCs, a ~$400B+ Farm Credit GSE, and the BaaS/neobank deposit layer - each carrying its own HTM/CRE/duration/credit risk, mostly outside the lens the 'banks look clean' read uses.

The same rate/credit common factor ([[macro-cross-sectional-analysis]], ~91%) that prices the bank HTM hole also prices credit-union securities, private-credit NAVs, pension alts, mortgage-REIT books, MMF/hedge-fund Treasuries, and farmland - so 'the banks are fine' understates a correlated, far larger pool that mostly shares the SAME Treasuries, agency MBS and self-marked NAVs. The order of magnitude (~$33-37T outside the FDIC lens) rivals the insured bank base itself.

non_claims: No claim any of these pools is insolvent or failing system-wide - credit unions and the FCS are well-capitalized in aggregate; the point is they are UNCOVERED by the usual FDIC lens and carry correlated risk.
Private-credit size is scope-dependent ($1.7T-$3T); the range is stated rather than a false-precise single number.
The ILC 'loophole' framing is a documented policy debate (ICBA et al.), not an assertion of wrongdoing by any ILC.
Foreign-bank-branch and FCS exact totals live in their primary releases; figures here are as-reported approximations.
Overlay edges are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: ~$2.43T NCUA parallel system; rising delinquency (103bp) + 6 failures 2025 (7-yr-high); own HTM/CRE risk, outside FDIC data
edges.note: OVERLAY: uncovered pool; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: ~$1.97T bank lending to NDFIs - risk migrated off-balance-sheet into manager-marked private credit (~$1.7-3T)
edges.note: OVERLAY: risk migration; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: ~$248B FDIC-insured but Fed-unsupervised charters (the loophole) - a parallel insured-deposit pool
edges.note: OVERLAY: supervision gap; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: ~$2T+ US branches/agencies of foreign banks - wholesale dollar-funding/repo node (Fed IBA), outside the FDIC set
edges.note: OVERLAY: offshore-dollar transmission; excluded from proofs

limits: Maps the credit-risk pools below or outside the FDIC bank lens, with real figures: credit unions ~$2.43T (NCUA; delinquency 103bp, 6 failures 2025 = 7-yr-high cluster); private credit/NDFIs ~$1.7-3T (BDCs ~$500B, evergreen $644B, bank-to-NDFI $1.97T); ILCs ~$248B (FDIC-insured/Fed-unsupervised loophole, 23 charters, largest ~$116B); the Farm Credit System GSE (~$188B farm-RE + ~$81B non-RE loans, YE2024); foreign-bank US branches ~$2T+ (Fed IBA); and the FDIC's suppressed problem-bank ASSET total (count-only since Q4 2024). The FDIC bank number is a floor; credit unions and private credit are the two biggest uncovered reservoirs, both showing rising stress. Overlay edges excluded from the proofs. source: see verification-log-equivalent sources + the rendered .md.

checks.result: $2.43T assets, NW 11.26%, delinquency 103bp Q4-2025; 6 failures Apr-Aug 2025 = largest in ~7yr (NCUA Q4-2025 / Ceto).
~$247.7B total (2025), 23 charters, largest ~$116.3B; FDIC-insured/Fed-unsupervised loophole (CRS R46489).
FCS ~$188B RE + ~$81B non-RE farm loans YE2024; foreign-bank US branches ~$2T+ (Fed IBA); FDIC problem-bank asset total suppressed since Q4-2024 (Wolf Street).
